models.methodology: The hackathon methodology steps explaining how events are structured.
models.hackathons: Individual hackathon events with details, results, and resources.
models.winningTeams: Teams that won or placed in hackathon competitions.

relationships: Each Hackathon produces multiple AIModels (linked via hackathonId)
Each Hackathon has WinningTeams with their projects
Each Hackathon has a BestPractices report linked from Toolbox
Photos belong to a specific Hackathon

methodology.title: Challenge Design
methodology.description: Local agricultural partners identify pressing challenges in crop production, livestock management, farm finance, and climate adaptation. Challenge briefs are crafted with input from agronomists and farmers.

methodology.title: Team Formation
methodology.description: Data scientists, developers, and agricultural domain experts form cross-functional teams. Participants are selected through an open application process.

methodology.title: Development Sprint
methodology.description: 48-72 hours of intensive building with mentorship from AI and agriculture experts. Teams develop working prototypes addressing the challenge.

methodology.title: Demo Day
methodology.description: Teams pitch their solutions to a panel of judges. Winners receive prizes, mentorship, and support to open-source their models.

hackathons.name: Nairobi AgriTech Hackathon
hackathons.tagline: AI solutions for crop science and precision farming in East Africa
hackathons.description: The Nairobi hackathon brought together 85 participants to develop AI solutions for crop yield prediction, plant disease detection, and soil health management for smallholder farmers in East Africa.

challengeBrief.title: AI for Smallholder Crop Science
challengeBrief.summary: Develop AI tools that help smallholder farmers predict yields, detect crop diseases early, and optimize soil fertility management.

winningTeams.description: Machine learning model predicting crop yields using weather and soil data for maize, wheat, and sorghum.
Mobile app for plant disease detection across East African crop varieties using computer vision.
Precision fertilizer recommendation system using soil sensor data and crop requirements.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for crop science and precision farming
2 agri-tech startups formed post-hackathon
Partnership with KALRO for crop and soil data access
15,000+ GitHub stars across models

hackathons.name: Lagos FoodSystems Hackathon
hackathons.tagline: AI solutions for supply chain and market access in West Africa
hackathons.description: Lagos hosted a hackathon addressing post-harvest losses and market access challenges in Nigerian agriculture. 120 participants developed AI models for cassava disease detection, farm-to-market logistics, and commodity price forecasting.

challengeBrief.title: AI for Food Systems & Market Access
challengeBrief.summary: Build AI tools that reduce post-harvest losses, optimize farm produce logistics, and improve market price transparency for Nigerian farmers.

winningTeams.description: Deep learning model detecting cassava mosaic and brown streak disease from smartphone photos.
Route optimization for perishable farm produce delivery in Nigerian cities.
Market price prediction for agricultural commodities across West African markets.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for food systems
Partnership with Federal Ministry of Agriculture for crop data
CassavaBlight Detector piloted in 5 cassava farming communities
FarmRoute adopted by 2 agricultural logistics startups

hackathons.name: Accra AgriFinance Hackathon
hackathons.tagline: Expanding agricultural financial access through AI
hackathons.description: The Accra hackathon focused on AI solutions for agricultural financial inclusion, targeting smallholder farmers without formal credit histories. 70 participants developed credit scoring, crop insurance, and market matching solutions.

challengeBrief.title: AI for Agricultural Finance
challengeBrief.summary: Create AI solutions that expand financial access for smallholder farmers using farm productivity data and mobile money patterns.

winningTeams.description: Alternative credit scoring combining satellite farm data with mobile money patterns.
Parametric crop insurance risk assessment using satellite and weather data.
Matching platform connecting farmers to buyers based on produce and location.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for agricultural finance
Regulatory sandbox partnership with Bank of Ghana for agri-lending
HarvestCredit piloted with 3 agricultural microfinance institutions
€500K in follow-on funding for winning teams

hackathons.name: Cairo Nile AgriTech Challenge
hackathons.tagline: AI for irrigation efficiency and climate resilience along the Nile
hackathons.description: The Cairo hackathon brought together 90 participants to develop AI solutions for Nile Basin irrigation management, agricultural drought prediction, and fraud detection in farm subsidy programs.

challengeBrief.title: AI for Nile Basin Agriculture
challengeBrief.summary: Develop AI tools that optimize irrigation in the Nile Delta, predict agricultural drought, and protect farm subsidy programs from fraud.

winningTeams.description: Smart irrigation scheduling for Nile Delta canal systems using real-time sensor data.
Fraud detection for agricultural subsidy disbursement and mobile lending.
Satellite-based drought prediction for agricultural planning in the Nile Basin.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for irrigation and climate resilience
Partnership with Egypt Ministry of Agriculture for canal sensor data
IrrigationFlow AI piloted in 3 Nile Delta irrigation districts
First North African agricultural AI hackathon in the program

hackathons.name: Dakar Sahel AgriHack
hackathons.tagline: AI solutions for pastoralism and water management in the Sahel
hackathons.description: Dakar's hackathon addressed agricultural challenges in the Sahel region including soil moisture monitoring, solar-powered irrigation optimization, and livestock disease detection for pastoral communities. 65 participants from Francophone Africa.

challengeBrief.title: IA pour l'Agriculture au Sahel
challengeBrief.summary: Développer des solutions IA pour la gestion de l'eau agricole, l'irrigation solaire et la santé animale dans la région sahélienne.

winningTeams.description: IoT-driven soil moisture and groundwater prediction for Sahel irrigation planning.
Solar pump output forecasting for off-grid irrigation systems.
Computer vision for livestock disease detection in pastoral herds.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for Sahel agriculture
Partnership with Senegal Ministry of Agriculture for field data
SahelWater Monitor deployed in 10 rural farming communities
First Francophone agricultural AI hackathon in the program

hackathons.name: Kigali AgriLivestock Summit
hackathons.tagline: AI for cash crops and livestock health in the Great Lakes region
hackathons.description: Rwanda's hackathon focused on AI for tea optimization, dairy cattle health monitoring, and agricultural credit scoring within the country's organized cooperative system. 55 participants developed solutions for Rwandan agricultural value chains.

challengeBrief.title: AI for Rwandan Agriculture
challengeBrief.summary: Build AI tools that optimize cash crop harvesting, monitor livestock health, and improve farmer access to credit through cooperative data.

winningTeams.description: Precision harvesting model for tea plantations using weather and growth patterns.
Digital credit scoring using cooperative transaction and harvest delivery data.
Wearable sensor analytics for early cattle disease detection in dairy herds.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for cash crops and livestock
TeaLeaf Optimizer piloted in 10 Rwandan tea cooperatives
Partnership with Rwanda Agriculture Board for crop data
50% of participants were women developers

hackathons.name: Johannesburg AgriAI Summit
hackathons.description: The Johannesburg summit was the program's flagship event, bringing together 150 participants from across Africa. Teams addressed grain market connectivity, frost prediction for vineyards, and poultry health monitoring for commercial farming.

challengeBrief.title: AI for African Agricultural Systems
challengeBrief.summary: Build AI solutions for grain market access, climate-smart farming, and livestock health monitoring across Southern Africa.

winningTeams.description: Supply-demand matching platform for grain trading across Southern Africa.
Micro-climate frost early warning system for Western Cape vineyards.
Automated poultry flock health screening from audio and image analysis.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for grain, climate, and livestock
GrainMarket Connect piloted with 3 grain trading cooperatives
€1M in follow-on funding for winning teams
Largest agricultural AI hackathon by participant count

hackathons.name: Casablanca MedAgri Hackathon
hackathons.tagline: AI solutions for tree crops and pest control in North Africa
hackathons.description: The Casablanca hackathon brought together 75 participants from Francophone North and West Africa to develop AI solutions for olive yield forecasting, agricultural supply chain optimization, and desert locust swarm prediction.

challengeBrief.title: IA pour l'Agriculture Méditerranéenne
challengeBrief.summary: Développer des solutions IA pour l'optimisation des cultures arboricoles, la prédiction des invasions acridiennes et la logistique agricole.

winningTeams.description: Olive tree yield prediction using climate and phenological data.
Predicting agricultural input purchase timing from diaspora remittance flows.
Desert locust swarm trajectory prediction and early alert system.

outcomes: 3 open-source AI models published for tree crops and pest control
Partnership with Morocco Ministry of Agriculture for crop data
OliveYield Forecast piloted with 20 olive cooperatives
Second Francophone agricultural hackathon in the program

pageContent.title: Hackathons
pageContent.descriptionTemplate: Our hackathon series brought together {participants}+ developers, data scientists, and agricultural experts to build {models} open-source AI models addressing African farming challenges.

emptyState.title: No hackathons found for the selected country.

methodologyContent.title: Our Hackathon Methodology
methodologyContent.description: A proven approach to building AI solutions that address real African agricultural challenges.
